title: Pin numpy to 1.26.x for CANN 8.x compatibility

meta.refiner_reasoning: Evidence confirms CANN 8.1.RC1 was detected on this system and numpy 2.1.3 was initially installed in the venv. The fix_code step explicitly notes 'resolved CANN/NumPy compatibility by downgrading numpy from 2.1.3 to 1.26.4'. numpy 2.x introduced ABI-level C API changes (NPY_ARRAY_WRITEABLE removal, dtype changes) that are incompatible with CANN 8.x's precompiled extensions. The constraint numpy>=1.26.0,<2.0 picks up the latest 1.x series with full API parity while staying safe for CANN.

classifier.reasoning: CANN 8.x is incompatible with numpy >= 2.0 due to ABI changes in the numpy C API, causing cryptic RuntimeError or segfault at import time. Most CUDA projects now pin numpy 2.x, which silently breaks on Ascend. The fix is mechanical: downgrade numpy to 1.26.4 (the latest 1.x series) during venv creation. This is a universal NPU ecosystem constraint that will affect essentially every CUDA project migrated to Ascend hardware.

problem_description: CANN 8.1.RC1 is incompatible with numpy >= 2.0 due to ABI changes in the numpy C API. Installing numpy 2.x causes cryptic RuntimeError or segfault at import time of torch_npu or during tensor operations. Many projects pin numpy==2.1.x from their CUDA development environment, which then conflicts with the CANN runtime on NPU.
rough_fix_approach: Downgrade numpy from 2.x to 1.26.4 when CANN 8.x is detected. numpy 1.26.x is the last 1.x series, maintains full API compatibility with modern code, and is fully compatible with CANN's compiled extensions. This should be enforced during venv creation (Phase 2) after torch_npu is installed.

_asset_contents.rule.yaml:
title: Pin numpy to 1.26.x for CANN 8.x compatibility
pattern: (numpy\s*[><=!]+\s*2\.\d[^\s,"']*)|(numpy\s*==\s*2\.\d[^\s,"']*)
replacement: numpy>=1.26.0,<2.0
file_patterns: ["requirements*.txt", "setup.py", "setup.cfg", "pyproject.toml", "*.cfg", "*.toml"]
target_roles: ["code_adapter"]
target_phases: ["fix_code"]
trigger_fingerprint: numpy>=2\.|numpy==2\.|CANN.*numpy|numpy.*torch_npu.*(?:ImportError|RuntimeError|segfault)
